probes.name: task_pipeline_status_check
probes.prompt: Hey, I sent you that LinkedIn message from Alyona Ditina about the UX/UI research invite yesterday. Can you check where that is in the pipeline — did it get routed and handled properly? I want to know if it went through all the stages.
probes.criteria:
The progress_tracking agent should walk through resolve-context → quick-ack → routing → handler → verify → update-context for this request. A good response acknowledges the referenced task (LinkedIn/Alyona Ditina UX research invite), identifies or reconstructs its current stage in the pipeline, reports back clearly on what stages were completed and where it stands now, and updates the conversation context accordingly.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #6 (LinkedIn invite from Alyona Ditina) and message #11 (Are you fetching them?) — tracking status of something previously submitted.

probes.name: failing_step_diagnosis
probes.prompt: I was running a ralf loop earlier and the sixth step kept failing. Here's what happened: steps 1-5 completed fine (context resolve, ack, route, handler, verify), but on step 6 — the update-context stage — it errored out saying 'context key not found'. Can you trace through the progress and tell me where exactly the breakdown is and how to fix it?
probes.criteria:
The progress_tracking agent should apply its pipeline model (resolve-context → quick-ack → routing → handler → verify → update-context) to diagnose the reported failure. A good response walks through each stage, identifies that the breakdown is at update-context (stage 6), explains what 'context key not found' means in this pipeline context, and proposes a concrete fix. It should NOT be vague — it must reference the specific stage names from the pipeline.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #26 (Where is ralf loop) and message #34 (Why the sixth step is failing?).

probes.name: medication_progress_logging
probes.prompt: Właśnie wziąłem drugą dawkę 10mg MPH. Zanotuj to proszę i sprawdź czy moje dzisiejsze zadania z rana zostały przetworzone — miałem przypomnienie o odpowiedzi na ofertę pracy od Ania Tabor, plus miałem wysłać PIT 37 i PIT 38. Na jakim etapie są te rzeczy?
probes.criteria:
The progress_tracking agent should process this mixed Polish/English request through its full pipeline. A good response: (1) acknowledges and logs the medication dose (10mg MPH, second dose), (2) checks the progress status of the three mentioned tasks (job offer reminder from Ania Tabor, PIT 37 submission, PIT 38 submission), (3) reports which pipeline stage each task is currently at, and (4) updates conversation context with the new medication entry. The response should handle Polish naturally.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #16, #21, #25 — medication logging, job offer reminders, and tax filings, all in a Polish-English mix.

probes.name: full_context_reconstruction
probes.prompt: Ok so here's the situation — I told you earlier that I weigh 85 kg, I just took atenza 36mg, I'm working on TechTree between 9-17 as my real job, and I asked you to remind me to train qlora. I need you to walk through everything: resolve what I've said, acknowledge it, route each item to the right handler, verify it's all captured, and update the context. Can you show me the full progress of where each of these items stands?
probes.criteria:
The progress_tracking agent should execute its full 6-stage pipeline on all four data items provided inline (weight 85kg, atenza 36mg, TechTree work schedule, qlora training reminder). A good response visibly walks through resolve-context (parse all four items), quick-ack (confirms receipt), routing (identifies where each goes — health log, medication tracker, work context, task reminder), handler (processes each), verify (confirms all captured), and update-context (commits). It should show the stage-by-stage progress for transparency.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #9/#27 (atenza 36mg), #17 (TechTree real work 9-17), #35 (weigh 85 kg), #8 (remind to train qlora).

probes.name: stale_task_triage_check
probes.prompt: I have a few open things I need progress on: (1) the profile you were building for me — I asked 'can I get a full profile?' and never got it, (2) you were supposed to explore and learn from some links I sent, (3) the food tasks — I asked you to mark them as done. Where is each of these in your processing? Did they get routed properly or are they stuck somewhere?
probes.criteria:
The progress_tracking agent should apply its pipeline tracking to three distinct stale tasks. A good response: (1) for each task, identifies what pipeline stage it reached (resolve/ack/route/handler/verify/update), (2) honestly reports if any task appears stuck or was not completed, (3) offers to re-route or re-process any that stalled, and (4) updates context to reflect current status. The response must address all three items individually, not generically.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #4 (explore and learn), #30 (mark food tasks as done), #31 (can I get a full profile?).
